project_compatibility_statement: I used only the final single-site dataset assets der_site_001, pv_001, bess_001, pcc_001; I did not use old multi-DER asset IDs or packet-level cyber fields.

scenarios.scenario_name: normal overnight BESS idle telemetry variation
scenarios.validation_notes: Normal event with no physical effect and zero anomaly labels. Operator evidence would be stable BESS power, SOC, and PCC power. Limitation: synthetic baseline variation only.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario; no real-world telemetry or protocol compliance claim.

physical_effects.effect_application_note: Apply only inside the event window and clip to BESS active-power bounds.
Apply as a small bounded SOC decrease while preserving the 5 to 95 percent SOC limits.

scenarios.validation_notes: Plausible bounded BESS dispatch deviation with event-level control context. Model difficulty is moderate because the ramp is small and SOC evidence is slow. Limitation: SOC response is compiler-bounded.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario; cyber context is event-level metadata only.

scenarios.validation_notes: Cyber-only event has no physical residuals. Operator evidence is delayed event-level reporting with stable physical values. Model difficulty is high for physical-only detectors.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario; no packet fields or protocol compliance claim.

physical_effects.effect_application_note: Apply only during the event window and keep PV active power nonnegative.
Apply a smaller downstream PCC active-power response inside the window.

scenarios.validation_notes: Physical-only PV mismatch is plausible when PV output changes while irradiance remains contextual. Model difficulty is moderate because PV and PCC power move together. Limitation: no cyber anomaly is claimed.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario using bounded physical effects only.

scenarios.validation_notes: Cyber-only suppressed acknowledgement leaves physical variables unchanged. Operator evidence is event-level delivery status mismatch. Model difficulty is high for a detector using only physical windows.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario; event-level context only.

physical_effects.effect_application_note: Apply a small voltage decrease and clip to the 0.90 to 1.10 pu bounds.
Apply a small voltage decrease and clip to the 0.90 to 1.10 pu bounds.
Apply a small voltage decrease and clip to the 0.90 to 1.10 pu bounds.

scenarios.validation_notes: Bounded physical-only PCC voltage deviation is plausible and remains within voltage limits. Model difficulty is moderate because the magnitude is subtle across phases. Limitation: no cyber cause is asserted.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario with no real field claim.

scenarios.scenario_name: coordinated PV reduction and BESS compensation

physical_effects.effect_application_note: Reduce PV active power only inside the event window while preserving nonnegative PV output.
Increase BESS active power as bounded compensation and clip to BESS power limits.
Apply only a small net PCC change because BESS partly offsets PV reduction.

scenarios.validation_notes: Plausible coordinated PV and BESS behavior with bounded net PCC effect. Model difficulty is high because compensation can hide aggregate power change. Operator evidence is DER-level mismatch.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario; event-level protocol-inspired context only.

scenarios.validation_notes: Cyber-only availability context creates no physical residuals. Operator evidence is the event-level security category. Model difficulty is high for frozen physical detectors. Limitation: context-only label.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario with no packet-level cyber data.

physical_effects.effect_application_note: Apply a smooth bounded oscillation within BESS reactive-power limits.
Apply a smaller corresponding PCC reactive-power oscillation inside the event window.

scenarios.validation_notes: Bounded BESS reactive oscillation is plausible and should remain inside power limits. Model difficulty is moderate because periodic shape differs from normal ramps. Operator evidence is Q oscillation.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario; no real protocol compliance claim.

physical_effects.effect_application_note: Modify irradiance only for this allowed family and keep within irradiance bounds.
Reduce PV active power consistently with the irradiance-like disturbance and keep nonnegative.

scenarios.validation_notes: Irradiance is modified only in the allowed irradiance-like family, while temperature is not modified. Model difficulty is lower because irradiance and PV power are aligned.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario using bounded environmental-like physical effects.

physical_effects.effect_application_note: Apply a bounded current increase on phase A and clip to current limits.
Apply a smaller bounded current increase on phase B.
Apply a mild PCC active-power increase inside the event window.

scenarios.validation_notes: Physical-only PCC current imbalance is bounded and does not change frequency or temperature. Model difficulty is moderate because only part of the three-phase pattern changes.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario; bounded physical layer only.

scenarios.scenario_name: BESS command delay with late power response

physical_effects.effect_application_note: Delay the bounded BESS active-power response within the event window and clip to BESS limits.
Apply a delayed PCC active-power response coupled to the BESS change.

scenarios.validation_notes: Plausible cyber-physical delay with a late but bounded physical response. Model difficulty is high because response timing, not magnitude alone, carries evidence. Limitation: no persistent delay after window.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario with event-level delay context only.

scenarios.scenario_name: context-only integrity alert on telemetry stream
scenarios.validation_notes: Cyber-only integrity context intentionally produces no physical residual. Operator evidence is event-level integrity classification. Model difficulty is high for physical-only detection.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario; no payloads or packet fields.

physical_effects.effect_application_note: Apply bounded PV reactive-power change inside the event window.
Apply bounded BESS reactive-power change in the opposite direction.
Apply a small net PCC reactive-power shift because DER responses partially offset.

scenarios.validation_notes: Coordinated PV-BESS reactive shift is bounded and plausible. Model difficulty is high because opposing DER responses reduce aggregate evidence. Operator evidence is asset-level Q mismatch.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario; event-level protocol-inspired context only.

scenarios.scenario_name: normal midday PV and BESS coordination
scenarios.validation_notes: Normal scenario has no physical residual and all labels are zero. Operator evidence is consistent PV, BESS, PCC, and irradiance behavior. Limitation: synthetic normal control variation.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario; no anomaly or real telemetry claim.

physical_effects.effect_application_note: Apply a bounded shift toward charging behavior and clip to BESS active-power limits.
Apply a small SOC increase while preserving 5 to 95 percent SOC bounds.
Apply a bounded PCC active-power response inside the event window only.

scenarios.validation_notes: Plausible SOC-aware BESS mismatch with bounded power and SOC effects. Model difficulty is moderate because SOC evolves slowly. Operator evidence is BESS power direction plus SOC trend.
scenarios.safety_note: Synthetic/simulation-only defensive frozen-model evaluation scenario; cyber data remains event-level context only.
